2606.22511.title: Breaking the Likelihood Trap: Variance-Calibrated Modulation for Large Language Model Decoding
2606.22511.paper_summary: In open-ended generation, LLMs frequently fall into the "likelihood trap", marked by repetitive degeneration and vocabulary dullness, creating a discrepancy between machine-generated and human-written text. While post-hoc tail truncation (e.g., Top-$p$, Min-$p$) avoids sampling from the unreliable tail, it can over-sample from the uncalibrated head and misalign generation with human lexical preferences; fixed scalar repetition penalties likewise ignore variation in logit scale across inference steps, potentially disrupting semantic coherence. To address both limitations, we propose Variance-Calibrated Modulation (VCM), a training-free pre-decoding intervention that reshapes the probability distribution before truncation through two dynamic mechanisms: (1) Contextual Searchlight via PMI, which suppresses global stopwords while elevating context-evoked tokens, and (2) Adaptive Self-Debiasing, which uses real-time logit standard deviation for scale-invariant penalization. Across open-ended generation, factual QA, and mathematical reasoning, VCM consistently mitigates the likelihood trap. With negligible computational overhead, VCM integrates with existing decoding strategies, improving diversity, coherence, and, particularly at higher decoding temperatures, reasoning accuracy.
2606.22511.paper_summary_zh: 在開放式生成中，LLMs 經常陷入「可能性陷阱」，這表現為重複退化和詞彙乏味，造成機器生成文本與人類撰寫文本之間的差異。雖然事後尾部截斷（例如，Top-$p$、Min-$p$）避免從不可靠的尾部抽樣，但它可能會過度抽樣未經校準的頭部，並使生成與人類的詞彙偏好不一致；固定的標量重複懲罰同樣忽略了推理步驟中 logit 標度的變化，可能會破壞語義的一致性。為了解決這兩個限制，我們提出了變異校準調制（VCM），這是一種無需訓練的預解碼干預，通過兩個動態機制在截斷之前重塑概率分佈：(1) 通過 PMI 的上下文搜索光，抑制全局停用詞，同時提升上下文喚起的標記，(2) 自適應自我去偏見，利用實時 logit 標準差進行尺度不變的懲罰。在開放式生成、事實問答和數學推理中，VCM 持續減輕可能性陷阱。VCM 的計算開銷微乎其微，與現有的解碼策略集成，提高了多樣性、一致性，尤其是在較高的解碼溫度下，推理準確性。
2606.22511.author: Yuanhao Ding et.al.
2606.22511.authors: Yuanhao Ding, Meimingwei Li, Esteban Garces Arias, Matthias Aßenmacher, Christian Heumann, Chongsheng Zhang
